generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance Heatbed : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element embedded within styrofoam box operating under PWM control for temperature regulation with thermal runaway prevention via over-temperature detection."
    DTDFVocab:actuates IncubatorEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:operationalRange ["20°C", "40°C"]
    DTDFVocab:controlProtocol "PWM"
]

instance Fan : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Low-power axial fan positioned for uniform airflow distribution within styrofoam box with variable-speed control via PWM or analog signals."
    DTDFVocab:actuates IncubatorEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:operationalRange ["0-100% speed"]
    DTDFVocab:controlProtocol "PWM/Analog"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer positioned within styrofoam box to measure internal content temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy over -55°C to +125°C range."
    DTDFVocab:measures Temperature
    DTDFVocab:communicationProtocol "1-Wire"
    DTDFVocab:dataTransmissionInterval "Configurable"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer deployed externally to monitor ambient conditions providing boundary condition data for model calibration."
    DTDFVocab:measures Temperature
    DTDFVocab:communicationProtocol "1-Wire"
    DTDFVocab:dataTransmissionInterval "Configurable"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance CenterTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer positioned at styrofoam box center to capture temperature gradients within the enclosure."
    DTDFVocab:measures Temperature
    DTDFVocab:communicationProtocol "1-Wire"
    DTDFVocab:dataTransmissionInterval "Configurable"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CenterTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HeatbedTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer positioned near heatbed to monitor localized thermal conditions for safety monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:measures Temperature
    DTDFVocab:communicationProtocol "1-Wire"
    DTDFVocab:dataTransmissionInterval "Configurable"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HeatbedTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor
    DTDFVocab:dataType "TimeSeries"
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol "1-Wire"
    DTDFVocab:fromDataTransmission DataAcquisitionEnabler
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
    DTDFVocab:dataType "TimeSeries"
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol "1-Wire"
    DTDFVocab:fromDataTransmission DataAcquisitionEnabler
]

instance CenterTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CenterTemperatureSensor
    DTDFVocab:dataType "TimeSeries"
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol "1-Wire"
    DTDFVocab:fromDataTransmission DataAcquisitionEnabler
]

instance HeatbedTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HeatbedTemperatureSensor
    DTDFVocab:dataType "TimeSeries"
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol "1-Wire"
    DTDFVocab:fromDataTransmission DataAcquisitionEnabler
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance GeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD-based representation capturing physical dimensions, shapes, and structural properties of styrofoam box enclosure and heatbed components."
    DTDFVocab:modelType "Geometric"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

instance ThermalBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation model described by differential equations analyzing heat transfer, temperature gradients, and thermal equilibrium within incubator environment."
    DTDFVocab:modelType "Behavioral"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FaultDetectionEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimationEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model predicting system behavior under varying environmental conditions including ambient temperature effects and boundary conditions."
    DTDFVocab:modelType "Analytical"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OptimizationEnabler
]

instance InternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series temperature data collected from internal sensors representing content temperature within styrofoam box for calibration and monitoring purposes."
    DTDFVocab:dataType "TimeSeries"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FaultDetectionEnabler
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series ambient temperature data collected from external sensors providing boundary condition data for environmental compensation in model calibration."
    DTDFVocab:dataType "TimeSeries"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalModel
]

instance CenterTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series temperature data from center sensor capturing internal temperature gradients for spatial analysis and model validation."
    DTDFVocab:dataType "TimeSeries"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CenterTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalBehaviorModel
]

instance HeatbedTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series temperature data from heatbed proximity sensor monitoring localized thermal conditions for safety monitoring and calibration."
    DTDFVocab:dataType "TimeSeries"
    DTDFVocab:fromData HeatbedTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelCalibrationEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FaultDetectionEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataAcquisitionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi-based system collecting sensor data via 1-Wire protocol and transmitting via RabbitMQ message broker for real-time processing."
    DTDFVocab:hardwarePlatform "Raspberry Pi"
    DTDFVocab:communicationProtocol "1-Wire/RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionService
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance ModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based calibration algorithms using SciPy's numerical operations to refine model parameters through non-linear least squares optimization."
    DTDFVocab:softwareLanguage "Python"
    DTDFVocab:algorithmLibrary "SciPy"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalBehaviorModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalModel
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService
]

instance FaultDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom algorithmic implementation for detecting anomalies and violations in system operation based on real-time sensor data and model predictions."
    DTDFVocab:softwareLanguage "Python"
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetectionService
]

instance StateEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithmic component assessing system condition and performance against safety and optimality thresholds using real-time data inputs."
    DTDFVocab:softwareLanguage "Python"
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService
]

instance SupervisoryControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated controller adjusting PT parameters or operational state based on detected deviations from target conditions."
    DTDFVocab:softwareLanguage "Python"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance SimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Virtual replication framework enabling predictive analysis of PT behavior under current and hypothetical operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:softwareLanguage "Python"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance OptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithms improving system performance through adaptive parameter adjustments based on real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:softwareLanguage "Python"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataCollectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time and historical monitoring service collecting sensor data from PT and transmitting to DT framework for processing."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureMonitoringInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides DataQualityAssuranceAction
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model refinement service updating DT parameters through non-linear least squares calibration against real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterAdjustmentAction
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection service identifying violations or suboptimal conditions in PT operation through real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ViolationAlertAction
]

instance StateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "System condition assessment service evaluating PT performance against safety and optimality thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemConditionInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceEvaluationAction
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Autonomous intervention service adjusting PT parameters or operational state based on detected deviations."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalAdjustmentInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomatedControlAction
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Virtual replication service enabling predictive analysis of PT behavior under current and hypothetical conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveAnalysisInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioTestingAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Performance tuning service improving system efficiency through adaptive optimization algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterTuningAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of internal and external temperature data against target profiles for operational monitoring."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of DT model accuracy through calibration metrics comparing predictions with real-time sensor data."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of system deviations or potential failures through pattern recognition in real-time data streams."
]

instance SystemConditionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive assessment of PT's operational state including safety and performance metrics against established thresholds."
]

instance OperationalAdjustmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommended adjustments to PT parameters or operational state based on current system conditions and performance targets."
]

instance PredictiveAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted system behavior under current and hypothetical operating conditions enabling proactive decision-making."
]

instance DataQualityAssuranceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Preprocessing steps including filtering and normalization applied to sensor data before integration into DT models."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated updates to model parameters through non-linear least squares optimization using real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ViolationAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggering alerts when detected violations exceed predefined safety or optimality thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AutomatedControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time adjustments to heatbed and fan operation parameters based on supervisory controller recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ScenarioTestingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of virtual experiments testing hypothetical changes to PT operation before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterTuningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adaptive optimization of system parameters to improve performance and efficiency based on real-time monitoring results."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C18: Environment/Component
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Thermal environment within styrofoam box enclosure containing biological samples with controlled temperature gradients."
    base:contains StyrofoamBox
    base:contains Heatbed
    base:contains Fan
    base:contains TemperatureSensors
]

instance StyrofoamBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Primary enclosure providing thermal insulation for the incubator system with integrated heatbed and fan components."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance TemperatureSensors : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Collection of DS18S20 digital temperature sensors deployed at multiple positions within and around the styrofoam box."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
    base:contains InternalTemperatureSensor
    base:contains ExternalTemperatureSensor
    base:contains CenterTemperatureSensor
    base:contains HeatbedTemperatureSensor
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin system is described through **control flows** that originate from the Digital Twin’s **algorithms** and **monitors**, which validate system performance and safety. The document highlights that when **violations** (e.g., operational deviations) are detected, **humans or other algorithms** may initiate changes to the **Physical Twin (PT)**. While the **command types** are not explicitly listed, the context implies **adjustments to system parameters** (e.g., fan speed, heatbed temperature) to restore optimal operation. **Control message formats** are not specified, but the interaction suggests **structured commands** (e.g., JSON or binary) to trigger actions. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not detailed, though the system’s reliance on **calibration algorithms** implies some form of **parameter verification** before command execution. **Feedback systems** are implied through the **monitoring** component, which provides real-time performance data to inform corrective actions. **Conflict resolution** is not explicitly addressed, but the document suggests a **human-in-the-loop** or **algorithm-driven** approach to resolve discrepancies. **Command execution confirmation** is inferred through the **monitoring** phase, where system performance is reassessed post-intervention."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates on a time scale defined by asynchronous, time-series data collection from the Physical Twin (PT) via sensors, where data is gathered *over time* rather than in real-time or with strict synchronization constraints. The system employs calibration algorithms to iteratively update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, but the exact synchronization frequency or latency requirements are not specified. The described workflow includes a monitoring loop where detected violations (e.g., safety or optimality deviations) trigger human or algorithmic intervention, implying a non-immediate feedback mechanism. No explicit mention of low-latency, near-real-time, or immediate twinning synchronization is provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a modular architecture pattern where **data** (from PT sensors), **models** (of CPS and environment), and **algorithms** (e.g., calibration, fault detection, state estimation, optimization) are integrated into a cohesive framework. Resource management is implicit, relying on the calibration algorithms to update models with the best parameter estimates, ensuring consistency between the PT and DT. System-wide coordination is achieved through a feedback loop: monitors validate performance against safety/optimality criteria, and violations trigger human or algorithmic interventions (e.g., parameter adjustments or control actions). The orchestration emphasizes real-time adaptability, where models and algorithms dynamically interact to maintain alignment with the PT’s operational state."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) spans multiple phases, beginning with **conceptualization and design**, where abstract models (e.g., CAD representations of physical components like car chassis or wheel dimensions) are developed to capture static and geometric properties. This phase transitions into **dynamic modeling**, where engineers construct physics-based models (e.g., differential equations for force dynamics) to simulate behavior under operational conditions. Post-development, the DT integrates **real-time data acquisition** from the Physical Twin (PT) via sensors, enabling **calibration and parameter estimation** through algorithms that refine models (e.g., updating Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environmental parameters). The lifecycle includes **monitoring and validation**, where the DT employs algorithms (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to verify system performance against operational constraints. **Optimization** is embedded as an iterative process, using data-driven insights to adjust PT configurations or operational policies. Transition management between phases relies on **model persistence**—retaining pre-deployment models in the DT to enable continuous comparison between simulated and actual PT behavior. Lifecycle governance adheres to **iterative refinement principles**, ensuring alignment between evolving PT requirements and DT fidelity, with explicit governance frameworks for model versioning, data provenance, and compliance with operational constraints (e.g., safety thresholds)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system prioritizes **model fidelity** and **validity** through structured methodologies:
- **Fidelity requirements**: Models must accurately represent both the **cyber** (e.g., control systems) and **physical** (e.g., structural dynamics) aspects of the PT and its environment, ensuring predictive simulations align with real-world behavior.
- **Validation and verification methods**: Models are validated against empirical data from sensors, while verification involves cross-checking analytical formulations (e.g., differential equations) with observed system responses. **Calibration algorithms** adjust model parameters to minimize discrepancies between predicted and actual behavior.
- **Uncertainty sources and quantification**: Uncertainties arise from sensor noise, environmental variability, and model simplifications. These are quantified through statistical analysis of sensor data and model residuals, enabling probabilistic assessments of system performance.
- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**: Monitoring algorithms (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) identify deviations from expected behavior, triggering corrective actions such as parameter recalibration or human intervention.
- **Quality assurance processes**: Continuous validation ensures models remain aligned with the PT’s evolving state. The DT framework enforces **closed-loop feedback** between data, models, and algorithms to sustain high fidelity, with human oversight for critical decisions (e.g., system adjustments during violations).
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s Technical Connection** relies on a **hybrid communication architecture** integrating **RabbitMQ** (an open-source message broker) and **1-Wire bus** protocols. RabbitMQ operates as the primary **asynchronous messaging middleware**, hosted on the Raspberry Pi, enabling decoupled communication between sensors (PT), controllers, and DT components (e.g., monitors, calibration algorithms). The 1-Wire bus facilitates **low-level sensor data aggregation**, allowing multiple DS18S20 temperature sensors to share a single communication line while maintaining individual addressability. Network topology is **star-topology-centric** (via RabbitMQ) with **point-to-point sensor connections** (1-Wire bus). Bandwidth and latency requirements are not explicitly quantified, but the system assumes **low-latency asynchronous messaging** (critical for real-time calibration and monitoring). Reliability mechanisms include **RabbitMQ’s persistent message queues** and **redundant sensor polling** (via the low-level driver). Security protocols are not detailed, though RabbitMQ supports **TLS/SSL encryption** (implied for sensitive data flows). The infrastructure ensures **end-to-end connectivity** between PT sensors, the Raspberry Pi-based controller, and DT algorithms via RabbitMQ’s publish-subscribe model.

Key protocols: **RabbitMQ (AMQP)**, **1-Wire bus (DS18S20)**.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. The document does not specify **hosting platforms** (e.g., cloud, edge, on-premise), **computational/storage requirements**, or **scalability mechanisms**. No mention is made of **deployment models** (e.g., containerized, virtualized) or **availability/reliability assurance** (e.g., redundancy, failover). Thus, this characteristic is **Not in Document**."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) incorporates a **safe protocol** embedded within the low-level driver to ensure the Physical Twin (PT) remains in a **safe operational state** under all conditions. The system employs **monitoring algorithms** that continuously assess whether the PT is performing **safely and optimally** by comparing real-time data against calibrated models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment. When deviations or violations are detected, the system triggers **corrective actions**, which may be automated or manually intervened by human operators. The DT framework does not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems**, but the **safety-critical nature** of the DT is emphasized through its reliance on **calibration algorithms** and **real-time monitoring** to prevent unsafe states. No explicit mention of **risk assessment approaches**, **fail-safe mechanisms**, or **threat mitigation strategies** is provided in the documents."
]
